data.subject: Executive search insights for your leadership priorities

body.content:
You don't often get email from cutbill@hortoninternational.com. Learn why this is important
 
 
 

The Pressure on Executive Leadership Is Rising

 

Hi Adam

 

As 2026 progresses, leadership teams are moving from planning to execution. The strategic themes shaping this year are now playing out in real time across markets and sectors.

 

To build on our earlier perspective, we have selected three insights that explore how organisations are strengthening leadership capability and positioning for sustainable performance.

 

Strategic planning with people at the centre
Effective organisations are embedding leadership pipeline development and talent strategy directly into business planning. This integrated approach strengthens long-term resilience and ensures leadership capability keeps pace with organisational ambition.


Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/2026-strategic-planning-blueprint/

 

Developing future-ready leaders
As leadership expectations evolve, adaptability, strategic thinking, and human-centred capability are becoming defining attributes of successful executives. Building a strong leadership pipeline is now a strategic priority rather than a reactive exercise.

 

Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/leadership-pipeline-2026/

 

Leading with purpose in a digital world
Technology continues to reshape organisations, but leadership impact is increasingly defined by trust, culture, and engagement. Leaders who balance innovation with purpose and people-first decision-making set the tone for sustainable performance.

 

Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/human-side-of-2026/

 

If you'd like to discuss how these trends are playing out in your industry or region, simply reply and we'll connect you with the right specialist from our team.

 

Best regards,

The Horton International Team

 

 

Crafting Leadership, One Vision At A Time.

body.content:
Hello,

Thank you for being a valued ThinkOn customer. Our team of Thinkers continuously works with our partners to ensure the reliability, stability, and performance you expect from ThinkOn services.

This notification is to inform you of an upcoming planned maintenance event being performed by our network partner, Crown Castle Fiber, which may impact certain services.

Location of Work: Columbus, OH

Scheduled Start Date & Time Scheduled End Date & Time Time Zone

4/28/2026 12:00 AM 4/28/2026 6:00 AM Eastern

Expected Customer Impact: Service Affecting

Expected Impact Duration: Up to 4 hours

ThinkOn engineers will closely monitor all activities throughout the maintenance window to ensure the work is completed successfully and to minimize any potential service disruption.

Should you have any questions, concerns, or comments about this service maintenance, please reach out to us at requests@thinkon.com or by calling 1-877-787-0306.

Thank you for your continued commitment to ThinkOn—our customers are our top priority. Together, we make data thrive.

From: Sergio Bernardou <sbernardou@centrilogic.com>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 11:51 AM
To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

The #1 Services CPQ Platform for IT Service Providers

What's New in ScopeStack — Q2 2026

 

This quarter we shipped nine updates — including an AI executive summary generator, a fully redesigned service editor, and real-time financial tracking. Here's what's live in your account.

 

✨ AI-Generated Executive Summaries

Users with the appropriate permission can now request an AI-generated executive summary for any project — written from the services you've already scoped. One click produces a polished, client-ready summary without starting from a blank page.

 

💾 Upgraded Service Editor with Auto-Save

The project service editor has been fully rebuilt — same powerful scoping, cleaner interface, and every change saves automatically. No more manual saves, no lost work.

 

⏳ Real-Time Financials in the Project Header

Revenue, cost, and profit now update live as you scope. See the financial impact of every change in real time — no refreshing, no switching views.

 

✅ Multi-Condition Approval Rules

Business approval levels can now combine multiple conditions — for example, trigger a review when revenue exceeds $10k and the project includes custom services. More precision, fewer unnecessary approvals.

 

📋 Vendor Resource Management

Add vendor resources directly to projects with permission controls over who can edit hourly cost and price. When a resource is removed from a project, it's automatically cleaned up across all associated services.

 

✏️ Cleaner Text Editor

The text editor now uses a single shared toolbar per page and every editable region is resizable. Less clutter, more room to write.

 

🎨 Redesigned Across the Platform

The updated design system now covers Approvals, Notes, Contacts, Locations, Work Breakdown, all Pricing sections (Overall, PS, MS, Product, Payment Credits), the Clients and Accounts list, and Users & Groups.

 

All updates are live in your account. Log in to see what's changed.

Subject: RE: Credit App | TD SYNNEX

 

You don't often get email from patrick.molen@tdsynnex.com. Learn why this is important

Hello Matthew, thank you for speaking with me. 

 

We are excited about the prospects of embarking on a Net Terms relationship with Aptum.

 

Per our conversation, please send the interim 2026 six month financials including the balance sheet, income statement, and if available, the statement of cash flows and we will make you folks our top priority for a review assessment. 

 

From what I have experienced, the PMSI typically comes into play when 1): you are changing banks.  Banks like to be in first place.  As long as your account is not very far past due with us and as long as the new bank is financing your growth(ie: Arbitrarily speaking, if former bank was extending Aptum a $10M line and the new bank is only extending a $25K line and TD Synnex has a $1M line, we would want to discuss) , we would subordinate our position behind the new bank.  Or 2): if you firm unexpectedly files for bankruptcy protection or 3): your firm is purchased by a Private Equity firm, etc. they may ask us to subordinate. 

 

Benefits of the PMSI

 

1): we can set a higher net terms line than we can without it

 

2): after some history of on time payments, the PMSI opens up our RSA and other programs.  RSA for example would allow us to accommodate large sales orders to strong end-users outside of your net terms line so that the large order does not tie up your internal line,  Fee is 1% of the purchase order for the first $120K with a maximum fee of $1,200 so the program works well with very large million dollar orders as there is no additional fee after $1,200.  Program entails your end user customer paying a bank lockbox that TD Synnex controls.  We can remain 100% invisible to your end user.  We receive payment at the bank lockbox we control and we then turnaround 24 hours and send your firm an eft/ach payment.

 

3): with some history of on time payments and a signed Amplify Addendum, the PMSI opens up our Amplify extended terms program for Net 45, Net 60 etc.

From: Sergio Bernardou <sbernardou@centrilogic.com>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 11:51 AM
To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

April 15, 2026

 

Enacting a Moratorium

Even as billions of dollars have been committed to development, the potential environmental and utility infrastructure impacts of large-scale data centers have given rise to public opposition. A bill enacted Tuesday by the Maine Legislature may impose the first statewide moratorium on new construction of larger facilities.
 
With some tech firms already laying off employees to make way for expanded artificial intelligence use, concerns have also surfaced about the impact of AI on employment. A new report from Newmark aims to separate fact from fiction when it comes to defining the future of office space in the coming era of AI.

TOP STORY

Maine May Become First State to Enact Moratorium on New Data Centers

The bill, which awaits the governor’s signature, would freeze approvals on construction of facilities requiring more than 20 megawatts of power until fall 2027

CONNECT CRE

Understanding the AI-Office Space Connection

A Newmark report explains likely employment scenarios as more companies utilize AI and their impact on office space

Lincoln Property Company JV to Develop Data Centers in Kansas City

Metrobloks is partnering with Lincoln on the project, to be built on land already zoned and powered for data center use

CONNECT MONEY

Amazon to Acquire Globalstar to Expand D2D Satellite Connectivity

The acquisition will enable Amazon’s Leo satellite network to deliver direct-to-device connectivity and expand coverage to areas beyond traditional wireless infrastructure

CONNECT CANADA CRE

Hello Matthew, thank you for speaking with me. 

 

We are excited about the prospects of embarking on a Net Terms relationship with Aptum.

 

Per our conversation, please send the interim 2026 six month financials including the balance sheet, income statement, and if available, the statement of cash flows and we will make you folks our top priority for a review assessment. 

 

From what I have experienced, the PMSI typically comes into play when 1): you are changing banks.  Banks like to be in first place.  As long as your account is not very far past due with us and as long as the new bank is financing your growth(ie: Arbitrarily speaking, if former bank was extending Aptum a $10M line and the new bank is only extending a $25K line and TD Synnex has a $1M line, we would want to discuss) , we would subordinate our position behind the new bank.  Or 2): if you firm unexpectedly files for bankruptcy protection or 3): your firm is purchased by a Private Equity firm, etc. they may ask us to subordinate. 

 

Benefits of the PMSI

 

1): we can set a higher net terms line than we can without it

 

2): after some history of on time payments, the PMSI opens up our RSA and other programs.  RSA for example would allow us to accommodate large sales orders to strong end-users outside of your net terms line so that the large order does not tie up your internal line,  Fee is 1% of the purchase order for the first $120K with a maximum fee of $1,200 so the program works well with very large million dollar orders as there is no additional fee after $1,200.  Program entails your end user customer paying a bank lockbox that TD Synnex controls.  We can remain 100% invisible to your end user.  We receive payment at the bank lockbox we control and we then turnaround 24 hours and send your firm an eft/ach payment.

 

3): with some history of on time payments and a signed Amplify Addendum, the PMSI opens up our Amplify extended terms program for Net 45, Net 60 etc.

data.subject: [Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026] La nouvelle réalité de la sécurité IA

body.content:
View email online​

AMERICAS

21 avril

9 h 00 (PDT) // 12 h 00 (EDT)

Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026

Sécuriser l’IA sans compromettre l’avenir

Inscrivez-vous maintenant

Bonjour Adam,

​

Les décisions en matière de sécurité ne se prennent rarement en une seule fois. Elles s’accumulent, et les choix initiaux peuvent limiter silencieusement ce qui sera possible par la suite.

​

Le Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026 explore comment les organisations abordent la sécurité alors que les nouvelles capacités évoluent plus rapidement que les modèles existants n’ont été conçus pour le supporter, avec la perspective de Neil MacDonald, Distinguished VP Analyst chez Gartner.

​

Lors du sommet, nous explorerons :

Quelles décisions en matière de sécurité de l’IA ne peuvent être prises qu’une seule fois

Où la visibilité et la gouvernance commencent à se fragiliser

Comment l’IA concentre les risques dans des zones inattendues

Comment avancer sans créer une exposition à long terme

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

SERGIO BERNARDOU

Project Management Consultant

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
